title: Print Mode
text: Print Mode Specify the data to print. All —prints the complete form component, including all data currently in the component, all occurrences in the hitlist, and all fixed text. If header and trailer frames have been defined, they are printed on the top and bottom of each printed page, respectively. If an entity frame does not show all occurrences, or a field frame does not show all the data it contains, the frame is expanded to show all the data. If all occurrences in the hitlist have not yet been fetched, read triggers are activates to fetch the data. Width definitions in the layout definition of a field are ignored. Screen —prints only the data that can be seen on the screen. Applicable only for character based user interface (CHUI). For GUI, it behaves the same as Component . Any data not visible in the component window is not printed. The dimensions of the component window are the Width and Depth component properties. Component —prints the data and fixed text on the entire form component. If the form component is larger than the form component window, the part of the form not currently visible is also printed. If the form component is larger than one page, any header or trailer which has been defined is printed once, in the same place as it is painted on the form component. Uniface does not expand field or entity frames. They are printed as is. Clear —similar to All, but clears data from memory immediately after it has been printed. The hitlist is also dropped. This causes $hits to decrease step by step as the print process progresses. This mode is recommended when printing large quantities of data (in batch mode or report component) because it improves performance. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
